steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Rogerson criticized the USGS for omitting considerations about Earth's curvature. The sentence that follows draws a conclusion from that criticism: the USGS-determined center was inaccurate. The blank must introduce a transition that signals a consequence flowing from a stated cause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — contrast signal (e.g. 'However'): signals opposition, not consequence. ✗
  • Choice B — concession signal (e.g. 'Admittedly'): signals partial agreement, not a causal result. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Hence': marks consequence — the exact signal needed since Rogerson's criticism about omitted considerations directly explains why the USGS center was inaccurate. ✓
  • Choice D — sequence signal (e.g. 'Then'): signals time order, not cause-and-effect. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
